summary: Completed comprehensive testing of F1 Intelligence MVP with all 5 modules: Analytics Studio, Race Deep Dive, Story Explorer, Rivalry Explorer, and GOAT Engine. Backend APIs 95% functional, frontend UI fully operational with excellent data visualization and user interactions.

flaky_endpoints.impact: Circuit profile pages may not load in Story Explorer

design_issues.issue: HTML hydration warnings in console for table elements
Chart width/height calculation warnings in console

passed_tests: Analytics Studio loads with KPI tiles showing 26.8K races, 1.1K wins, 3.4K podiums, 30.4% DNF rate
Analytics Studio Drivers and Constructors tabs display statistics tables correctly
Analytics Studio chart visualizations render properly with driver performance data
Race Deep Dive loads race selector and displays 2024 Bahrain Grand Prix details
Race Deep Dive position-by-lap chart renders with driver tracking badges
Race Deep Dive shows race winner (Verstappen), pole sitter, fastest lap, and DNF count
Rivalry Explorer loads Hamilton vs Verstappen comparison with H2H statistics
Rivalry Explorer displays qualifying, race finish, points, wins, and podiums comparisons
GOAT Engine loads with weight configuration sliders and preset buttons
GOAT Engine leaderboard API returns ranked drivers with scores and breakdowns
Story Explorer loads Hamilton driver profile with career statistics
Story Explorer displays career timeline chart, best seasons, and signature circuits
Story Explorer facts generation creates interesting driver statistics
Navigation between all 5 modules works correctly
/api/stats/overview returns valid KPI data structure
/api/goat/leaderboard returns properly formatted GOAT rankings
/api/rivalry/1/830 returns Hamilton vs Verstappen H2H data
/api/driver/1/profile returns Hamilton's comprehensive career data
/api/facts/generate creates driver-specific interesting facts
All metadata endpoints (seasons, drivers, constructors, circuits, races) functional
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix circuit profile endpoint (/api/circuit/6/profile) returning 520 error. Consider addressing React hydration warnings in table components for cleaner console output.
